questions.question: What is the main purpose of the insertion sort algorithm?
options: To find the median of a list
To sort a list in ascending order
To find the sum of a list
To find the product of a list
questions.answer: To sort a list in ascending order

questions.question: How does insertion sort work for a list of numbers?
options: By sorting in descending order and then reversing
By repeatedly comparing and swapping adjacent elements
By using a temporary array to store the sorted elements
By using recursion to sort the list
questions.answer: By repeatedly comparing and swapping adjacent elements

questions.question: What is the first step in the insertion sort algorithm?
options: Comparing the first element with the rest
Comparing the last element with the rest
questions.answer: Comparing the first element with the rest

questions.question: Why does insertion sort have a time complexity of O(n^2)?
options: Because it has to compare each element with every other element
Because it has to make a copy of the list
Because it has to use a temporary array
Because it has to use recursion
questions.answer: Because it has to compare each element with every other element

questions.question: Can insertion sort be used for sorting strings?
options: No, only numbers can be sorted
Yes, strings can be sorted using a modified version of insertion sort
No, it's only for lists of integers
Yes, strings can be sorted using the same algorithm as for numbers
questions.answer: Yes, strings can be sorted using a modified version of insertion sort

questions.question: What is the algorithm used by insertion sort for comparing and swapping elements?
questions.answer: Bubble sort

questions.question: Why is insertion sort a stable sorting algorithm?
options: Because it always preserves the order of equal elements
questions.answer: Because it always preserves the order of equal elements

questions.question: Can insertion sort be used for sorting linked lists?
options: Yes, insertion sort can be used for linked lists with modifications
Yes, insertion sort can be used for linked lists without modifications
No, sorting linked lists is not possible
questions.answer: Yes, insertion sort can be used for linked lists with modifications

questions.question: What is one of the required capabilities of an intelligent system according to the text?
options: Understand a given situation or challenge
Select actions that help achieve a desired goal
questions.answer: Understand a given situation or challenge

questions.question: What does search help the agent decide in AI problem solving?
options: What to wear for the day
questions.answer: Which action to take next

questions.question: How does an AI system formulate a problem in complex environments?
options: Analyzing possible actions and selecting the best one
questions.answer: Analyzing possible actions and selecting the best one

questions.question: What is the role of search in AI problem solving mentioned in the text?
options: To rely only on predefined rules
To explore the state space using search algorithms

options: Route planning systems that find the shortest or fastest path between locations
questions.answer: Route planning systems that find the shortest or fastest path between locations

questions.answer: Abstraction

questions.answer: Weather, radio, traffic, or companions

questions.answer: The AI system could operate in complex and dynamic environments

questions.question: What is the main function of mitochondria in a cell?
questions.answer: To generate energy

questions.question: Where are mitochondria located in a cell?
questions.answer: In the cytoplasm

questions.question: Why do mitochondria have their own DNA?
options: So they can store their own identity
Because they are not related to the cell
questions.answer: To replicate themselves

questions.question: Why do athletes need more mitochondria?
options: Because they need more energy to run
Because they need more oxygen to breathe
questions.answer: Because they need more energy to run

questions.question: What would happen if a cell has damaged mitochondria?
options: The cell would produce more energy
questions.answer: The cell would die

questions.question: How do mitochondria generate energy?
questions.answer: Through cellular respiration

questions.question: Why are mitochondria important for our survival?
options: Because they generate energy for the body
questions.answer: Because they generate energy for the body

questions.question: How do mitochondria adapt to changes in energy demand?
questions.answer: By increasing their number

questions.question: What is binary search?
options: A way to search for a word in a dictionary
A method to sort a list of numbers
A technique to find an element in a sorted list
A formula to solve a math problem
questions.answer: A technique to find an element in a sorted list

questions.question: Why do we use binary search?
options: It's the fastest way to search for an element
It's the easiest way to search for an element
It's because we run out of elements to search
It's because the list is not sorted
questions.answer: It's the fastest way to search for an element

questions.question: What happens when we perform a binary search?
options: The algorithm looks at every element in the list
The algorithm compares the target element to the middle of the list
The algorithm splits the list into two halves and repeats
The algorithm gives up and returns nothing
questions.answer: The algorithm splits the list into two halves and repeats

questions.question: Why do we need the list to be sorted for binary search?
options: So the algorithm can find the element more easily
So the algorithm can avoid duplicate elements
So the algorithm can skip over empty spaces
So the algorithm can perform a different search
questions.answer: So the algorithm can find the element more easily

questions.question: What happens when the target element is not in the list?
options: The algorithm returns the target element
The algorithm tells us the element is not in the list
The algorithm says it can't find the element
questions.answer: The algorithm tells us the element is not in the list

questions.question: How many comparisons does binary search make on average
options: The number of elements in the list
Log2 of the number of elements in the list
The number of elements in the list squared
questions.answer: Log2 of the number of elements in the list

questions.question: Why is binary search more efficient than linear search?
options: It eliminates half of the elements in each iteration
questions.answer: It eliminates half of the elements in each iteration

questions.question: What is the advantage of using binary search over linear search in a real-life scenario?
options: It's better for large lists and large datasets
questions.answer: It's better for large lists and large datasets
